data.body:
PASS-1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS.

T3 applies only when a plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a never-before-used capability. This plan integrates exclusively with rebar's internal, already-used event-store machinery — no external surface is introduced. Per T3's ANTI-FP clause ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), this is a clean PASS.

Every capability the plan asserts was verified present and exercised in-tree, so none is "asserted-but-unverified":
- Reducer-skip / forward-compat: `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` + `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` already lists `REVIEW_RESULT` (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:44).
- Compaction PRESERVES the event: documented (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:14-16) and pinned by test (tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py:110 `test_unknown_event_file_survives_compaction`).
- `fsck` WARN on event types newer than the running binary: `is_unknown_newer_type` (src/rebar/reducer/_version.py:47) + Check 5 (src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py:152-175).
- Preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout, readers-before-writers: existing rebar pattern (CLAUDE.md:301; docs/event-schema.md:77).

No capability gap; no SPIKE warranted on T3 grounds. Affirmations: technical_feasibility (achievable as described, on existing primitives); named mechanisms MATCH the codebase; auth/HTTPS preconditions n/a (no external endpoint). No T3 findings.
